rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Fluor Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 2 (the Privette doctrine), taken off calendar as to issues 3-5 (strict products liability, false representation and punitive damages) since those matters have been dismissed, and denied as to issue 1 (exposure to asbestos-containing materials). There is a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant at the Union Oil Refinery between 1960 and 1966. Assuming without deciding that some variant of the sophisticated use/intermediary defense might potentially apply to the negligence and contractor liability claims against defendant, defendant has not established either the knowledge or constructive knowledge of either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos or the knowledge of defendant regarding what was known or should have been known by either plaintiff or his employer about the hazards of asbestos, so any such defense has not been established as a matter of law. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, AUGUST 19, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Defendant Raymond Interior Systems-North motion for summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Cupertino Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The depositions of Mr. Cantley and plaintiff create a triable fact regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Cantley¿s deposition are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 2 (premises owner/contractor liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 1 and 3 (false representation and punitive damages) since those matters have been dismissed. Plaintiff¿s characterization of his claims for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability shows that those claims are indistinguishable from each other and thus it would be confusing to permit plaintiff to assign two different labels to the same claim. The more accurate and less confusing label is negligence. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Thermal Mechanical, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Harold Beasley Heating & Plumbing, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to the causes of action for strict products liability and false representation and the prayer of punitive damages since those matters have been dismissed and denied as to the causes of action for negligence and premises owner/ contractor liability. On the basis of the evidence presented by both parties the court cannot say as a matter of law that defendant acted reasonably with respect to plaintiff or that defendant should not have known of the health hazards of the asbestos-containing materials that it exposed plaintiff to. The objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. There are triable facts whether defendant¿s employees were at the Hewlett Packard site in 1959 and at the Standard Oil site in 1961-65. Plaintiff¿s deposition and declaration conflict with the declaration of Mr. Miles on these issues and it is not for the court on a summary motion to choose whom to believe. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE BILES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Nibco Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff¿s deposition and declaration considered with defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSSERT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has sustained its burden of production and plaintiff has presented insufficient evidence to create a triable fact regarding asbestos content of any product attributable to defendant that the plaintiff was exposed to. The objections to Mr. Cohen¿s declaration are sustained. =(220/HEK)

The declaration of Jack Sturges considered with defendant¿s GO 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Sturges¿ declaration are overruled. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT G BAKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, August 19, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of George Tyler creates a triable issue regarding plaintiff¿s exposure to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Tyler¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s request for a continuance is denied. If the tentative ruling is adopted, the court will revisit defendant¿s ex parte application to take the deposition of Mr. Tyler. =(220/HEK)
